intent: Machine-readable MVP feature blueprint for a mobile-first SPA with simple backend, persistent storage, and selective agentic LangGraph functionality.

goal.summary: A single mobile-first SPA that helps one person or household manage the full sell-and-buy home journey in one place.
primary_outcomes: Turn messy information into decision-ready summaries

feature_groups.name: Core product structure
feature_groups.description: Authentication, project setup, dashboard, and foundational app controls.

features.name: Role permissions: owner / partner-collaborator / read-only collaborator
Encrypted storage for sensitive notes and financial assumptions
Single home screen with sale status, buy status, next tasks, saved properties, latest messages/logs, and financial snapshot
Quick-add actions for property, note, call log, document upload, financial scenario, and task

feature_groups.name: Sell-side features
feature_groups.description: Manage the current property sale from planning through settlement.

features.name: Create a Sell Current Home project
Store property details for the home being sold
Capture ownership and basic house details
Track sale strategy: auction / tender / deadline sale / price by negotiation / fixed price
Store each agent’s name, agency, contact details, appraisal range, proposed commission, marketing estimate, recommended sale method, and notes
Record why an agent was shortlisted or rejected
Best case / expected / worst case sale outcome
Document checklist for title, LIM, builder reports, rates info, and body corporate info if applicable
Track document/checklist state as not started / in progress / complete / waiting on third party
Record offer terms: price / conditions / settlement date / deposit
Track sale milestone: listed / open homes underway / offer received / under contract / unconditional / settled

feature_groups.name: Buy-side features
feature_groups.description: Manage property discovery, due diligence, and offer workflows for the purchase.

features.name: Create a Buy Next Home project
Store listing details: address, price/guide, bedrooms, bathrooms, parking, land area, floor area, property type, listing method
Watchlist status: researching / inspecting / shortlisted / offer candidate / rejected
Track report requests: LIM / title / builder’s report / rental appraisal / body corporate docs
Mark risk severity: low / medium / high
Create purchase scenarios for a property
Conditions selector: finance / builder’s report / LIM / sale of existing home / solicitor approval
Track milestone: researching / due diligence / preparing offer / under offer / conditional / unconditional / settled

feature_groups.name: Financial modelling
feature_groups.description: Calculate affordability, equity, sale proceeds, purchase costs, and multi-scenario comparisons.

features.name: KiwiSaver / other available funds if relevant
Model one-off transaction costs relevant to jurisdiction
Best case / base case / stress case
Sensitivity testing for sale price drops, rate rises, fee increases, and delayed sale
Can I afford this if… modelling

feature_groups.name: Communications and record-keeping
feature_groups.description: Central contact records, communication history, and searchable activity timeline.

features.name: Store key contacts: agents, brokers, solicitors, valuers, inspectors, bank contacts, movers
Link contacts to sale or purchase project
Manual log of text messages / chat summaries
Link a communication to a property, contact, task, or decision
Chronological timeline across properties, contacts, offers, tasks, uploaded files, and notes
Filter timeline by project or property

feature_groups.name: Research and knowledge capture
feature_groups.description: Save, organise, tag, and search external research and property-specific references.

features.name: Categorise research by topic: suburb research / sale process / legal / lending / property checks / market trends
Write notes against each saved item
Link research items to a specific property
Link comparable sales notes to a suburb or property
Store vendor/agent claims and verification notes

feature_groups.name: Tasks, reminders, and workflow
feature_groups.description: Task management, templates, and workflow-driven reminders.

features.name: Auto-generate checklist tasks from workflow templates
Mark task status: to do / in progress / waiting / done

feature_groups.name: Documents and file storage
feature_groups.description: Upload, categorise, and organise files linked to projects, properties, and contacts.

features.name: Link files to properties, contacts, or projects
Folder-like views by sale / purchase / property / contact

feature_groups.name: Agentic LangGraph functionality
feature_groups.description: Selective agent workflows that add real value by summarising, retrieving, comparing, and planning using user-owned data.

features.name: Summarise saved articles, brochures, and reports
Extract key points from uploaded documents
Produce a plain-English summary of a LIM, builder report, or title
Surface likely risks and unanswered questions
Suggest follow-up questions for agent / solicitor / broker
Turn rough notes into clean call summaries
Draft follow-up emails or message replies
Extract action items from logged communications
Suggest next steps after a meeting or inspection
Generate comparison summaries between shortlisted homes
Highlight inconsistencies between user criteria and current preference
Identify missing information before making an offer
Explain scenario differences in plain English
Warn when a scenario looks fragile
Suggest cheaper or safer alternative scenarios based on saved assumptions
Build a weekly action plan from all open tasks
Summarise current project state for quick review
Semantic search across notes, docs, communications, research, and saved properties
Ask natural-language questions over stored data
Citation-backed answers linked to original records
Agent memory scoped to project and property

feature_groups.name: Search and filtering
feature_groups.description: Global search, saved searches, recent items, and operational filters.

features.name: Global search across all app content
Filter by property / suburb / project / contact / tag / date
Quick filters for overdue tasks, shortlisted properties, missing documents, outstanding questions, and active financial scenarios

feature_groups.name: Decision support
feature_groups.description: Structured decision records and generated decision summaries.

features.name: Capture why the decision was made
Which property is best fit? summary
Can I afford this safely? summary
What must happen before I can make an offer? summary

feature_groups.name: Reporting and export
feature_groups.description: Export scenario data, summaries, logs, and project packs.

features.name: Backup export of all structured data

feature_groups.name: Mobile UX requirements
feature_groups.description: Mobile-first interaction requirements needed for the MVP to feel usable in the field.

features.name: Quick capture flows for notes, photos, and calls
Minimal friction to add data immediately after inspections or calls

not_in_mvp: Full real-time listing aggregation from every property portal
Automated valuation model presented as authoritative
Full chatbot front-end as the main UX
Full document OCR pipeline for every file type on day one

must_have_feature_groups: User account and secure project setup
Agentic summarisation and retrieval over user-stored data

product_principles: The app is a control centre, not a portal clone.
Store structured data first; use agents to transform and retrieve that data.
Keep agentic flows narrow, explainable, and citation-backed.
Optimise for fast capture immediately after calls, inspections, and decisions.
Financial clarity is a first-class feature, not a nice-to-have.
langgraph_usage_rules: Only use agentic workflows where they save real effort or reduce decision friction.
Do not let the assistant invent facts; answers should cite app records.
Keep memory scoped to project and property.
Use retrieval over user-owned notes, documents, and communication logs.
Prefer summary, extraction, comparison, and next-step recommendation workflows.
